items.headline: City Council to review draft urban water master plan
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission will present an overview of Sunnyvale's Draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan. This plan guides the city's water management and drought response strategy.

items.headline: Spring cycle community events and neighborhood grants considered
items.summary: City Council will consider applications for the 2026 Community Events and Neighborhood Grant Program in the spring cycle. In-person public comment will be heard on these grant awards.

items.headline: City Council to review residential electrification programs
items.summary: The council will hear a status update and consider proposed principles for residential electrification assistance programs designed to help Palo Alto comply with air quality regulations.

items.headline: City Council to vote on new rules for accessory dwelling units
items.summary: The council will consider amending city code to address state law changes and prior council direction regarding accessory and junior accessory dwelling units (ADUs/JADUs). This affects residents interested in adding smaller units to their properties.

items.headline: Stream Corridor Protection rules under review
items.summary: The council will consider amendments to Palo Alto's stream corridor protection ordinance. The update addresses environmental protections for waterways in and around the city.

items.headline: Auto sales use proposed at 975 W. El Camino Real
items.summary: Planning Commission considered a special development permit to allow auto sales at an existing commercial property on El Camino Real. The project involves site modifications including repaving, restriping, and landscape upgrades, but no building expansion or exterior changes.

items.headline: 195-unit housing project permitted near Berryessa Transit Station
items.summary: A $32 million multi-family development received a building permit for 825 Berryessa Station Way, adding significant housing near the Berryessa BART station. The project aligns with San José's transit-oriented development goals in the Berryessa area.

items.headline: Water bill assistance pilot program launches for San José customers
items.summary: The City Council approved a Financial Assistance Pilot Program to help water customers with bills. This new program is designed to provide relief to residents struggling with water service costs.
